title: The Sorting Feast
prose: The Great Hall is lit by what must be a thousand floating candles, and above them the ceiling shows a clear, star-scattered sky — bewitched, someone whispers behind you, to look like the sky outside. {{upbringing-reaction}}

When the Hat calls out {{house}}, the table under that colour erupts, and you make your way over on legs that don't quite feel like your own, sliding onto the bench among a row of strangers who are already making room.

Then the food arrives — all at once, and far too much of it. Roast potatoes, a joint of beef going pink at the middle, Yorkshire puddings the size of fists, peas, carrots, a gravy boat that never seems to run dry, and at the end of the table a tower of treacle tart that vanishes almost as fast as it's served.

choices.label: Introduce yourself to the person next to you.
Say nothing yet — just watch the Hall and the other houses.
